title: Patching Resources with kubectl patch
description:
## Patching Resources with `kubectl patch`

`kubectl patch` lets you surgically update specific fields of a live resource without editing the full manifest. It supports three patch strategies: `merge`, `json`, and `strategic`.

**Your task:**

A Deployment named `patchme` is running in the `default` namespace with 1 replica.

1. Use `kubectl patch` to update its replica count to **3**
2. Use `kubectl patch` to add a label `env=staging` to the Deployment's **pod template** (`.spec.template.metadata.labels`)

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get deploy patchme -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}'
kubectl get deploy patchme -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels}'  
```

hints.title: Patch replicas using merge patch
hints.body: Use `--type=merge` with a JSON snippet targeting the field you want to change.

hints.title: Patch pod template labels
hints.body: To add labels to the pod template, target spec.template.metadata.labels in your patch body.
